notes:
LAYER ORDER CONFIRMED (actual depth values observed in file):
  terrain       depth -100  (_terrainContainer, _renderTerrainLayer)
  fog           depth -75   (_fogContainer, new — this cycle)
  paths         depth -50   (_pathContainer, _renderPathLayer)
  landmarks     depth -25   (_landmarkContainer, _renderLandmarkLayer)
  node bases    depth  0    (nodeBaseG, inline in create())
  node circles / icons / labels  depth 1  (per-loc loop in create())
  vignette      depth 1000  (_renderVignette)

FOG DOES NOT BLOCK NODE CLICK HANDLERS:
  Node circles are set interactive at: circle.setInteractive({ useHandCursor: unlocked }) in the per-location loop in create(). The fog Graphics object (_fogGraphics) is never passed to setInteractive(). Phaser.GameObjects.Graphics has no hit area by default (unlike Image/Sprite which auto-register on their texture bounds). Without an explicit setInteractive() call, no InputPlugin hit-test is registered for the Graphics object, so all pointer events fall through to the node circles at depth 1. No additional configuration is required.

TILE SIZE:
  DISCOVERY_TILE_SIZE = 32 px (imported from discoverySystem.js). Fog tiles are 32x32 px, matching the discovery grid exactly so fog cell boundaries align with discovered/undiscovered cell boundaries.

FOG DEPTH CHOSEN:
  -75. Terrain sits at -100; paths sit at -50. The midpoint -75 gives fog a clean slot above terrain but below all interactive and decorative layers. Landmarks at -25 and nodes at 0/1 are both above fog, so decorated geography and node icons always render on top of fog.

FRAME-TIME ESTIMATE FOR redrawFog() ON FRESH SAVE (everything fogged):
  Map parchment ~900x615 px with 32 px tiles = ceil(900/32) x ceil(615/32) = 29x20 = 580 tiles. On a fresh save every tile is undiscovered, so all 580 tiles receive a fillRect call. A single fillStyle(0x000000, 1.0) call is made once before the loop; Phaser Graphics batches all fillRect calls into one WebGL draw group. The terrain layer (same tile count + blended RGB per tile + heightmap sampling) renders in ~5-8 ms per the debug console log. Fog is strictly simpler (one colour, no blending math, no transparency). Estimated: ~0.5-2 ms on Chrome desktop. Well within the 16 ms budget. The redrawFog() method itself emits a console.debug timing line so actual measurements are available at runtime.
